sections.subtitle: Match Overview and Team Standings
sections.text: Wolverhampton, sitting at 13th in the league with 41 points, host Brighton & Hove Albion, who are in a stronger 10th position with 52 points. This matchup at Molineux Stadium in Wolverhampton promises to be a competitive clash. Brighton have shown better consistency this season, but Wolverhampton will be eager to capitalize on their home advantage. With referee Michael Oliver officiating, who averages 3.5 yellow cards per game, we can expect a tightly controlled encounter. Wolverhampton’s recent form at home and Brighton’s away performances will play a significant role in determining the outcome.

sections.subtitle: Recent Head-to-Head History
sections.text: The last matchup between these sides on October 26, 2024, ended in an exciting 2-2 draw, with Wolverhampton’s Matheus Cunha earning Player of the Match honors with an impressive 8.3 Sofascore rating. Over the last 5 encounters, the results are balanced with 1 win for Wolverhampton, 2 draws, and 2 wins for Brighton. Head-to-head trends show a high likelihood of goals, as 6 out of the last 8 matches have seen over 2.5 goals. Additionally, both teams have had issues with clean sheets, with Brighton failing to keep one in their last 3 games against Wolverhampton. The trend of more than 4.5 cards in all of the last 5 head-to-heads also suggests a physical battle ahead.

sections.subtitle: Key Players to Watch - Wolverhampton
sections.text: Wolverhampton’s attack is spearheaded by Matheus Cunha, a forward ranked 3rd in the league for average Sofascore rating at 7.65 and 2nd for times fouled per game at 2.3. Cunha has scored 15 goals this season against an expected 7.94, showing significant overperformance, and added 6 assists. Despite missing key forward Jørgen Strand Larsen, who has netted 13 goals, Wolverhampton can rely on players like Rayan Aït Nouri, a midfielder with 4 goals and 7 assists. In-form over the last 5 matches, Cunha continues to shine with a 7.92 average rating and 3 goals. Contributions from lesser-known names like Jean-Ricner Bellegarde, with 6 assists, could also prove vital.

sections.subtitle: Key Players to Watch - Brighton & Hove Albion
sections.text: Brighton’s offensive threat comes from Danny Welbeck, who has scored 9 goals against an expected 8.12, supported by Kaoru Mitoma with another 9 goals. Defender Adam Webster stands out with a league rank of 3rd for aerial duels won at 77.3%, which could be crucial against Wolverhampton’s physical style. Pervis Estupiñán has been a standout in past matchups against Wolverhampton, averaging a 7.62 Sofascore rating over the last 5 games with 1 goal and 1 assist. However, Brighton will miss forward João Pedro, who has 10 goals and 6 assists. Players like Yankuba Minteh, with 6 goals, could step up in his absence.

sections.subtitle: Team Statistics and Tactical Insights
sections.text: Wolverhampton have scored 51 goals this season, ranking 12th, but underperformed their expected goals by -9.9956, placing them 19th in over/underperformance. They excel in tackles per game at 21.17, ranking 1st in the league, and interceptions at 8.06 per game, ranking 3rd. Brighton, meanwhile, show a knack for late goals, with 26.79% of their goals coming between the 76th and 90th minutes. Wolverhampton are strong in ground duels, winning 51.54% to rank 6th, but struggle with aerial duels at 48.09%, ranking 16th. Both teams average low corners, with Wolverhampton at 3.54 per game, ranking 20th, suggesting a game with fewer set-piece opportunities.

sections.subtitle: Streaks and Trends
sections.text: Brighton are on a streak of 6 games without a clean sheet, with 5 of their last 6 matches seeing over 2.5 goals and both teams scoring in their last 5 away games. Wolverhampton have been first to score in 5 of their last 7 home matches, which could give them an early edge. Head-to-head data indicates Brighton often score first in these matchups, doing so in 6 of the last 8 encounters. Both teams have a consistent trend of less than 10.5 corners in recent games, with Wolverhampton at 6 of 8 and Brighton at 9 of 10. Expect a high card count, as their last 5 direct clashes have all seen over 4.5 cards.

sections.subtitle: Injuries and Squad Depth
sections.text: Wolverhampton will miss forward Jørgen Strand Larsen, who has contributed 13 goals, and Hee-Chan Hwang, impacting their attacking depth. Brighton are also without key forward João Pedro, with 10 goals, and midfielder Georginio Rutter, potentially weakening their frontline. Wolverhampton’s squad depth will rely on players like Gonçalo Guedes and Pablo Sarabia to fill the gaps. Brighton can turn to Danny Welbeck and Yankuba Minteh to lead the attack despite the absences. These missing players might lead to a more cautious approach from both sides.

sections.subtitle: Interesting Facts and Venue Impact
sections.text: Molineux Stadium has historically been a tough ground for Brighton, with Wolverhampton managing to secure points in recent home games against them. Wolverhampton rank 3rd in the league for the percentage of goals assisted at 76.47%, showcasing their teamwork in attack. Brighton have hit the woodwork 11 times this season, ranking 12th among the unluckiest teams, which could haunt them again. Wolverhampton’s strength in fast breaks, averaging 1.09 per game to rank 8th, could exploit Brighton’s defense. With Michael Oliver’s tendency for 0.14 red cards per game, a sending-off isn’t entirely out of the question in a heated contest.
